- Migrated app -> apps/marketing (git mv, history preserved); scaffolded apps/funnel (:3001) consuming @pbh/ui.
- Packages: @pbh/ui (shared primitives + cn), @pbh/tokens (Tailwind4 theme.css), @pbh/types (handoff-token payload), @pbh/config (eslint base + tsconfig presets).

NOT done (deferred per user): base CI (.github workflow) and Vercel project link + Ignored Build Step. Vercel linking overlaps pbh-bws.5. Marketing 'tinacms build' needs TinaCloud creds/env (pbh-bws.5). new_value:
{"notes":"Refresh/resume gap (follow-up, tied to auth — pbh-bws.18/FT18 Clerk session):

Consent step shipped on branch tuily/pbh-bws.4 (commit e5341f8) as a single-page client stepper (StepFlow.tsx). Step position + userId live in CLIENT MEMORY only. On a mid-funnel page refresh the client resets to step 1 (signup); since the account is already persisted server-side, re-submitting the same email hits the users_email_unique guard -> "email already registered" dead end, stranding the user before consent.

Docs do NOT mandate surviving an in-flow refresh, but the funnel is built around server-persisted checkpoints + email-based recovery (FT16 abandonment/resume emails), so this is a real drop-off gap. Decision for now: ship as-is (option 1). Proper fix rides with auth/session: on /get-started, derive the resume step server-side from existing state (account exists? consent rows exist? payment?) instead of client memory. Cheap stopgap if needed sooner: persist {stepIndex,userId} to sessionStorage/cookie (client-trusted, same caveat as the hidden userId field).

Also note: userId is passed to the consent server action via a client-trusted hidden field — replace with session-derived user once auth lands."}

old_value: {"id":"pbh-ro2","title":"Don't re-POST enrollSubject for active enrollments (started-state orphaning)","description":"Linus (Dan) confirmed re-POSTing enrollSubject is idempotent only for 'assigned' enrollments; for 'started' it mints a new enrollment id + redirect, orphaning in-progress work and the eventual report. The API exposes no per-enrollment status to distinguish the two. Fix: serve the stored redirect for active enrollments instead of re-POSTing; keep listEnrollments only to tell active from completed-pending.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"brizolla.tuily@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-06-29T17:24:12Z","created_by":"tuily","updated_at":"2026-06-29T17:24:12Z"}

Fix: serve the stored redirect for active enrollments instead of re-POSTing; keep listEnrollments only to tell active from completed-pending.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"brizolla.tuily@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-06-29T17:24:12Z","created_by":"tuily","updated_at":"2026-06-29T17:24:12Z"}

{"id":"pbh-bws.22","title":"Build: Stripe Payment Element + HSA/FSA","description":"Payment step inside SAQ-A boundary; HSA/FSA support.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"tuily","owner":"\u003cmark@stenquist.com\u003e","created_at":"2026-06-01T17:38:39Z","created_by":"Mark Stenquist","updated_at":"2026-07-01T16:58:52Z","external_ref":"https://linear.app/visualboston/issue/PBH-16"}

new_value:
{"notes":"HSA/FSA flag (is_hsa_fsa) — decision + open question (2026-07-01, tuily):

KEEP the payments.is_hsa_fsa column. FT9 (docs: product/features/FT-signup-payment-flow.md) makes 'HSA/FSA card flagged correctly on receipt' a GO-LIVE acceptance criterion, so the per-payment flag is required.

BUT the detection mechanism FT9 assumes is NOT possible. Confirmed against current Stripe docs:
- Stripe has NO API-level HSA/FSA detection. Support page: 'Stripe does not provide API-level detection for HSA/FSA cards.'
- PaymentMethod.card exposes no health-benefit field and NO full BIN/IIN — only last4. funding is credit|debit|prepaid|unknown (prepaid is a weak, unreliable proxy).
- So FT9's 'HSA/FSA card detected on the Stripe Customer object' cannot be implemented — Stripe surfaces no such data. This resolves the open standup item (2026-06-08: validate Stripe HSA/FSA config steps).
- Stripe's own receipt shows nothing about HSA/FSA and only itemizes invoice/subscription payments (ours is a one-off PaymentIntent) — hence the custom Resend receipt (FT11/D11) is required.
- Acceptance itself is purely account config: HSA/FSA-eligible MCC (8011/8099). SIGIS/IIAS only applies to merchants selling products, not a medical service — so we're on the simpler MCC path.

PROPOSED populator: user self-select checkbox ('I'm paying with an HSA/FSA card') → is_hsa_fsa. SAQ-A-safe (no PAN), self-reported (not financial truth). funding==prepaid could prefill only.

OPEN — needs PM sign-off: is a self-select checkbox acceptable to satisfy the FT9 receipt-flag requirement, given Stripe can't auto-detect? Checkbox NOT built yet (deferred out of the test-mode demo)."}

old_value: {"id":"pbh-ro2","title":"Don't re-POST enrollSubject for active enrollments (started-state orphaning)","description":"Linus (Dan) confirmed re-POSTing enrollSubject is idempotent only for 'assigned' enrollments; for 'started' it mints a new enrollment id + redirect, orphaning in-progress work and the eventual report. The API exposes no per-enrollment status to distinguish the two. Fix: serve the stored redirect for active enrollments instead of re-POSTing; keep listEnrollments only to tell active from completed-pending.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"tuily","owner":"brizolla.tuily@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-06-29T17:24:12Z","created_by":"tuily","updated_at":"2026-06-29T17:25:23Z","closed_at":"2026-06-29T17:25:23Z","close_reason":"Serve stored redirect for active enrollments instead of re-POSTing; corrected enrollSubject/listEnrollments docstrings per Linus feedback. typecheck+lint green."}

{"id":"pbh-dmx","title":"Create Stripe Customer from stored user data at payment time","description":"We already store firstName, lastName, email, zip, stateOfResidence on users, and a stripeCustomerId column exists but is never populated. {"id":"pbh-bws.22","title":"Build: Stripe Payment Element + HSA/FSA","description":"Payment step inside SAQ-A boundary; HSA/FSA support.","notes":"HSA/FSA flag (is_hsa_fsa) — decision + open question (2026-07-01, tuily):

KEEP the payments.is_hsa_fsa column. FT9 (docs: product/features/FT-signup-payment-flow.md) makes 'HSA/FSA card flagged correctly on receipt' a GO-LIVE acceptance criterion, so the per-payment flag is required.

BUT the detection mechanism FT9 assumes is NOT possible. Confirmed against current Stripe docs:
- Stripe has NO API-level HSA/FSA detection. Support page: 'Stripe does not provide API-level detection for HSA/FSA cards.'
- PaymentMethod.card exposes no health-benefit field and NO full BIN/IIN — only last4. funding is credit|debit|prepaid|unknown (prepaid is a weak, unreliable proxy).
- So FT9's 'HSA/FSA card detected on the Stripe Customer object' cannot be implemented — Stripe surfaces no such data. This resolves the open standup item (2026-06-08: validate Stripe HSA/FSA config steps).
- Stripe's own receipt shows nothing about HSA/FSA and only itemizes invoice/subscription payments (ours is a one-off PaymentIntent) — hence the custom Resend receipt (FT11/D11) is required.
- Acceptance itself is purely account config: HSA/FSA-eligible MCC (8011/8099). SIGIS/IIAS only applies to merchants selling products, not a medical service — so we're on the simpler MCC path.

PROPOSED populator: user self-select checkbox ('I'm paying with an HSA/FSA card') → is_hsa_fsa. SAQ-A-safe (no PAN), self-reported (not financial truth). funding==prepaid could prefill only.

OPEN — needs PM sign-off: is a self-select checkbox acceptable to satisfy the FT9 receipt-flag requirement, given Stripe can't auto-detect? Checkbox NOT built yet (deferred out of the test-mode demo).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"tuily","owner":"<mark@stenquist.com>","created_at":"2026-06-01T17:38:39Z","created_by":"Mark Stenquist","updated_at":"2026-07-01T18:35:21Z","external_ref":"https://linear.app/visualboston/issue/PBH-16"}

{"notes":"PIVOT (2026-07-08): funnel uses Stripe EMBEDDED CHECKOUT (ui_mode: "embedded", EmbeddedCheckoutProvider/EmbeddedCheckout), NOT the Payment Element. Confirmed with team. No card-on-file — the Stripe Customer object was dropped (one-off PaymentIntent; VB scope ends at the handoff redirect, so downstream card-reuse storage is out of scope). Docs D3/D7/FT8 + phase-1-vs-phase-2 updated to match. Checkout UI locale pinned to "en".

HSA/FSA flag (is_hsa_fsa) — decision + open question (2026-07-01, tuily):

KEEP the payments.is_hsa_fsa column. FT9 (docs: product/features/FT-signup-payment-flow.md) makes 'HSA/FSA card flagged correctly on receipt' a GO-LIVE acceptance criterion, so the per-payment flag is required.

BUT the detection mechanism FT9 assumes is NOT possible. Confirmed against current Stripe docs:
- Stripe has NO API-level HSA/FSA detection. Support page: 'Stripe does not provide API-level detection for HSA/FSA cards.'
- PaymentMethod.card exposes no health-benefit field and NO full BIN/IIN — only last4. funding is credit|debit|prepaid|unknown (prepaid is a weak, unreliable proxy).
- So FT9's 'HSA/FSA card detected on the Stripe Customer object' cannot be implemented — Stripe surfaces no such data. This resolves the open standup item (2026-06-08: validate Stripe HSA/FSA config steps).
- Stripe's own receipt shows nothing about HSA/FSA and only itemizes invoice/subscription payments (ours is a one-off PaymentIntent) — hence the custom Resend receipt (FT11/D11) is required.
- Acceptance itself is purely account config: HSA/FSA-eligible MCC (8011/8099). SIGIS/IIAS only applies to merchants selling products, not a medical service — so we're on the simpler MCC path.

PROPOSED populator: user self-select checkbox ('I'm paying with an HSA/FSA card') → is_hsa_fsa. SAQ-A-safe (no PAN), self-reported (not financial truth). funding==prepaid could prefill only.

OPEN — needs PM sign-off: is a self-select checkbox acceptable to satisfy the FT9 receipt-flag requirement, given Stripe can't auto-detect? Checkbox NOT built yet (deferred out of the test-mode demo)."}

KEEP the payments.is_hsa_fsa column. FT9 (docs: product/features/FT-signup-payment-flow.md) makes 'HSA/FSA card flagged correctly on receipt' a GO-LIVE acceptance criterion, so the per-payment flag is required.

BUT the detection mechanism FT9 assumes is NOT possible. Confirmed against current Stripe docs:
- Stripe has NO API-level HSA/FSA detection. Support page: 'Stripe does not provide API-level detection for HSA/FSA cards.'
- PaymentMethod.card exposes no health-benefit field and NO full BIN/IIN — only last4. funding is credit|debit|prepaid|unknown (prepaid is a weak, unreliable proxy).
- So FT9's 'HSA/FSA card detected on the Stripe Customer object' cannot be implemented — Stripe surfaces no such data. This resolves the open standup item (2026-06-08: validate Stripe HSA/FSA config steps).
- Stripe's own receipt shows nothing about HSA/FSA and only itemizes invoice/subscription payments (ours is a one-off PaymentIntent) — hence the custom Resend receipt (FT11/D11) is required.
- Acceptance itself is purely account config: HSA/FSA-eligible MCC (8011/8099). SIGIS/IIAS only applies to merchants selling products, not a medical service — so we're on the simpler MCC path.

PROPOSED populator: user self-select checkbox ('I'm paying with an HSA/FSA card') → is_hsa_fsa. SAQ-A-safe (no PAN), self-reported (not financial truth). funding==prepaid could prefill only.

OPEN — needs PM sign-off: is a self-select checkbox acceptable to satisfy the FT9 receipt-flag requirement, given Stripe can't auto-detect? Checkbox NOT built yet (deferred out of the test-mode demo).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"tuily","owner":"<mark@stenquist.com>","created_at":"2026-06-01T17:38:39Z","created_by":"Mark Stenquist","updated_at":"2026-07-08T14:12:19Z","external_ref":"https://linear.app/visualboston/issue/PBH-16"}

{"id":"pbh-ggr","title":"Booking StepFlow (5 Figma designs) — marketing-site modal, end-to-end","description":"Implement the 5 Figma booking/assessment StepFlow designs as a MODAL sequence on the marketing site, full end-to-end (real account, consent, Stripe payment, DB writes, Linus enrollment).

Figma: https://www.figma.com/design/SppKdzsaH6rQ14u90UpNSq/Primary-Brain-Health

DESIGN -> STEP MAPPING (all 5 already exist as funnel DEMO code, being re-homed to marketing):
- 360-2502  Landing booking section 'Start With a Brain Health assessment & Consultation' (First/Last/Email + Includes checklist + $149 / HSA-FSA note). Ref: apps/funnel .../signup/SignupForm.tsx + marketing IntakeForm block.
- 360-2854  Modal 1 'Welcome, {name}' details (DOB, gender, ZIP, phone, education, concerns). Ref: apps/funnel .../details/DetailsForm.tsx.
- 466-245 / 360-3214  Modal 2 'Almost there!' terms; scroll-to-enable -> 'Continue With Payment'. Ref: apps/funnel .../consent/ConsentForm.tsx.
- 360-3226  Modal 3 'Payment' — Stripe Embedded Checkout. Ref: apps/funnel .../payment/PaymentStep.tsx.

DECISIONS (from user):
- Flow lives ENTIRELY on the marketing site, presented as a MODAL sequence.
- Funnel /get-started stepper was DEMO scaffolding — not the production surface (retire it).
- Scope = full end-to-end.

BACKEND RECOMMENDATION — consolidate server logic into marketing via shared workspace packages (not copy-paste, not cross-app API):
- @pbh/db       Drizzle schema + migrations + Neon client (from apps/funnel/src/db)
- @pbh/payments Stripe server helpers + Checkout Session + webhook (from apps/funnel/src/lib/stripe)
- @pbh/linus    enrollment client (from apps/funnel/src/lib/linus)
- Re-home the 4 step server actions (createAccount, completeProfile, recordConsent, createAssessmentCheckoutSession/finalizeCheckoutSession) as MARKETING server actions calling those packages.
Rejected: marketing-calls-funnel-API (cross-origin CORS/cookie/iframe-Stripe friction); copy-paste rebuild (duplication/drift).

SHARED UI -> promote to @pbh/ui (shared UI only, never business logic): StepHeader, FieldError, Label, field/textarea class constants, 'Myself / Someone Else' segmented control, styled <select>.

OPEN ITEMS (non-blocking):
- Does the funnel keep /assessments + /login? If yes -> they consume @pbh/db/@pbh/linus; if funnel fully retired -> fold packages into marketing.
- Design tokens: Figma uses teal (#006e8a primary/CTA, #f5f3ee input bg, #224b60 Includes card) vs codebase Cognitive Sanctuary (navy #041632, forest #446558). Confirm whether teal is new brand primary or maps to existing tokens (see pbh-bws.39).

SUB-WORK CHECKLIST:
[ ] Extract @pbh/db, @pbh/payments, @pbh/linus from apps/funnel
[ ] Promote shared UI primitives to @pbh/ui
[ ] Build modal StepFlow state machine + 5 screens on marketing (open, transitions, terms-scroll gating, validation)
[ ] Tina landing block for 360-2502 (booking entry + Includes + $149)
[ ] Re-home 4 server actions to marketing; wire Stripe Embedded Checkout + webhook + Linus enrollment end-to-end
[ ] Reconcile design tokens
[ ] QA: test-mode payment through the modal (account -> consent row -> Stripe test card -> enrollment) + webhook backstop

RELATES TO: pbh-bws.17 (signup/consent/payment surfaces — was 'no Figma', now superseded by these designs on marketing), pbh-bws.20 (landing page), pbh-bws.21 (account creation + consent), pbh-bws.15 (component-library extension from V1), pbh-bws.24 (signed-token handoff), pbh-bws.39 (Stripe Appearance/brand tokens).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"brizolla.tuily@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-07-08T17:39:34Z","created_by":"tuily","updated_at":"2026-07-08T17:39:34Z"}

{"id":"pbh-ggr","title":"Booking StepFlow (5 Figma designs) — marketing-site modal, end-to-end","description":"Implement the 5 Figma booking/assessment StepFlow designs as a MODAL sequence on the marketing site, full end-to-end (real account, consent, Stripe payment, DB writes, Linus enrollment).

Figma: https://www.figma.com/design/SppKdzsaH6rQ14u90UpNSq/Primary-Brain-Health

DESIGN -> STEP MAPPING (all 5 already exist as funnel DEMO code, being re-homed to marketing):
- 360-2502  Landing booking section 'Start With a Brain Health assessment & Consultation' (First/Last/Email + Includes checklist + $149 / HSA-FSA note). Ref: apps/funnel .../signup/SignupForm.tsx + marketing IntakeForm block.
- 360-2854  Modal 1 'Welcome, {name}' details (DOB, gender, ZIP, phone, education, concerns). Ref: apps/funnel .../details/DetailsForm.tsx.
- 466-245 / 360-3214  Modal 2 'Almost there!' terms; scroll-to-enable -> 'Continue With Payment'. Ref: apps/funnel .../consent/ConsentForm.tsx.
- 360-3226  Modal 3 'Payment' — Stripe Embedded Checkout. Ref: apps/funnel .../payment/PaymentStep.tsx.

DECISIONS (from user):
- Flow lives ENTIRELY on the marketing site, presented as a MODAL sequence.
- Funnel /get-started stepper was DEMO scaffolding — not the production surface (retire it).
- Scope = full end-to-end.

BACKEND RECOMMENDATION — consolidate server logic into marketing via shared workspace packages (not copy-paste, not cross-app API):
- @pbh/db       Drizzle schema + migrations + Neon client (from apps/funnel/src/db)
- @pbh/payments Stripe server helpers + Checkout Session + webhook (from apps/funnel/src/lib/stripe)
- @pbh/linus    enrollment client (from apps/funnel/src/lib/linus)
- Re-home the 4 step server actions (createAccount, completeProfile, recordConsent, createAssessmentCheckoutSession/finalizeCheckoutSession) as MARKETING server actions calling those packages.
Rejected: marketing-calls-funnel-API (cross-origin CORS/cookie/iframe-Stripe friction); copy-paste rebuild (duplication/drift).

SHARED UI -> promote to @pbh/ui (shared UI only, never business logic): StepHeader, FieldError, Label, field/textarea class constants, 'Myself / Someone Else' segmented control, styled <select>.

OPEN ITEMS (non-blocking):
- Does the funnel keep /assessments + /login? If yes -> they consume @pbh/db/@pbh/linus; if funnel fully retired -> fold packages into marketing.
- Design tokens: Figma uses teal (#006e8a primary/CTA, #f5f3ee input bg, #224b60 Includes card) vs codebase Cognitive Sanctuary (navy #041632, forest #446558). Confirm whether teal is new brand primary or maps to existing tokens (see pbh-bws.39).

SUB-WORK CHECKLIST:
[ ] Extract @pbh/db, @pbh/payments, @pbh/linus from apps/funnel
[ ] Promote shared UI primitives to @pbh/ui
[ ] Build modal StepFlow state machine + 5 screens on marketing (open, transitions, terms-scroll gating, validation)
[ ] Tina landing block for 360-2502 (booking entry + Includes + $149)
[ ] Re-home 4 server actions to marketing; wire Stripe Embedded Checkout + webhook + Linus enrollment end-to-end
[ ] Reconcile design tokens
[ ] QA: test-mode payment through the modal (account -> consent row -> Stripe test card -> enrollment) + webhook backstop

RELATES TO: pbh-bws.17 (signup/consent/payment surfaces — was 'no Figma', now superseded by these designs on marketing), pbh-bws.20 (landing page), pbh-bws.21 (account creation + consent), pbh-bws.15 (component-library extension from V1), pbh-bws.24 (signed-token handoff), pbh-bws.39 (Stripe Appearance/brand tokens).","notes":"OPEN ITEMS RESOLVED (2026-07-08):
1. Funnel /assessments + /login STAY — the funnel is likely to become the authenticated app (post-purchase). So marketing = public site + booking modal flow; funnel = the app. Both consume shared packages -> extraction into @pbh/db / @pbh/payments / @pbh/linus is confirmed (do NOT fold into marketing).
2. Design tokens DEFERRED — Figma designs are not finalized; skip the teal-vs-Cognitive-Sanctuary token reconciliation for now. Build against existing @pbh/tokens; revisit once designs lock. (Drop the 'Reconcile design tokens' checklist item from scope for this pass.)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"brizolla.tuily@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-07-08T17:39:34Z","created_by":"tuily","updated_at":"2026-07-14T17:01:42Z","closed_at":"2026-07-14T17:01:42Z","close_reason":"All child issues delivered: shared @pbh/* packages extracted, step components promoted to @pbh/ui, modal StepFlow + Tina landing block on marketing, server actions re-homed with Stripe/webhook/Linus wired end-to-end, and E2E test-mode payment verified. Design-token reconciliation is OUT OF SCOPE for this epic (tracked separately under pbh-bws.39). Shipped via PR #13 -> staging."}

{"id":"pbh-bws.43","title":"Linus API integration tests (unit + mocked HTTP/DB)","description":"The Linus API integration is the funnel's highest-stakes external seam (register subject + enroll in cognitive assessments post-payment) and is currently UNTESTED. Add tests covering apps/funnel/src/lib/linus/* and apps/funnel/src/app/assessments/register-and-enroll.ts.

Depends on pbh-bws.42 (vitest harness must exist first).

Two layers:

1. Pure-unit (no network/DB):
   - extractReportData (client.ts): base64 reportData out of the array report shape.
   - buildRegisterInput + MissingDateOfBirthError (build-register-input.ts).
   - toSexAssignedAtBirth mapping + OTHER default.
   - getCampaigns env switch (campaigns.ts) via VERCEL_ENV (sandbox vs prod ids).
   - getLinusConfig missing-var error message (env.ts).

2. Mocked HTTP/DB (higher value):
   - client.ts: mock fetch to cover getAccessToken token caching/refresh (60s safety margin), bearer + User-Agent headers, and LinusApiError {status, body} thrown on non-2xx.
   - register-and-enroll.ts concurrency/idempotency logic (mock the DB layer):
     - ensureRegistered claim-win / deferred / error outcomes and retryOnContention behavior (webhook throws->500->Stripe redelivers vs client shows success).
     - storeParticipantId unique-violation (23505 / PgErrorCode.UniqueViolation) fallback to already-stored id.
     - resolveEnrollments 4-step ladder, esp. the invariant: NEVER re-POST an active/started enrollment (Linus enrollSubject mints a new one for 'started', orphaning the report).

Note: no HTTP-mocking pattern exists in the repo yet - establish one (vitest vi.mock / vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...)); it becomes the template for future funnel integration tests.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"brizolla.tuily@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-07-08T17:16:39Z","created_by":"tuily","updated_at":"2026-07-08T17:16:39Z"}

new_value: Fixed: stable #intake anchor on the booking block + hero Button/next-link hash-scroll workaround.
Root cause was @pbh/booking never registered as a Tailwind @source, so space-y-6 was never generated. Registered it and split the row gap (16px) from the CTA gap (24px) per Figma 751:1482.
StickyActions pins the submit button in the modal's scroll area with a blurred overlay; bottom padding moved off the modal body so the bar pins flush and does not jump at the end of scroll.
Merged in PR #15: stable #intake anchor on the booking block + explicit hero scroll (next/link doesn't jump for same-page hashes).

{"id":"pbh-1o5","title":"Hero/header/footer CTAs don't scroll to the booking form","description":"Every 'Book a Consultation' CTA targets #intake, but BlockRenderer derived section ids by slugifying the block headline, so the booking block rendered as #start-with-a-brain-health-assessment-and-consultation.
